atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the `@tailwind` directive to insert Tailwind's `base`, `components`, `utilities` and `variants` styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @theme
atDirectives.description: Use the `@theme` directive to define your project's custom theme variables for Tailwind CSS v4.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use the `@layer` directive to tell Tailwind which 'bucket' a set of custom styles belong to.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use the `@apply` directive to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use the `@config` directive if you need to load an external configuration file.

atDirectives.name: @source
atDirectives.description: Use the `@source` directive to specify the path to your content files.

atDirectives.name: @plugin
atDirectives.description: Use the `@plugin` directive to include external Tailwind plugins.

atDirectives.name: @utility
atDirectives.description: Use the `@utility` directive to define a custom utility class.

atDirectives.name: @custom-variant
atDirectives.description: Use the `@custom-variant` directive to define a new variant.
